summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering HR policy updates, fire department matters including a fire levy resolution, Station 18 construction updates, and road maintenance agreements. Key actions included approving HR policy revisions for EEO compliance, authorizing a 4.4 mil fire/EMS tax levy for the ballot, approving a memorandum of understanding with the firefighters union, and entering agreements for road paving grants and road salt purchases.
decisions: Approved HR policy additions and revisions for EEO compliance
Accepted resignation of part-time fitness trainer Paul Hilliard effective July 12
Approved Resolution R-012-2024 transferring $47,239.85 from Levy Fund 2193 to Firehouse Construction Fund 4901
Approved Resolution R-013-2024 to proceed with a 4.4 mil fire and EMS tax levy
Approved memorandum of understanding with IAFF Local 3932
Approved Resolution R-014-2024 declaring firefighter gear rack as surplus and authorizing donation to Monroe Township Fire Department
Authorized Township Administrator to enter agreement with Choice One Engineering for OPWC grant for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving in 2025
Authorized Township Administrator to enter agreement with Cargill Salt for road salt through SWAP 4G program
action_items: Chief Myers to bring motion to accept $41,761 employee retention grant at next meeting
Chief Myers to coordinate Congre and MSP presentation for August 13 trustees meeting on Station 18
Resolution R-013-2024 fire levy to be submitted to Board of Elections by next day (ahead of August 7 deadline)
Director Meadows to work with Choice One Engineering on OPWC grant application for Gibson Road and Belfast Road
Director Meadows to finalize agreement with Cargill Salt for road salt purchase
Donna Rossick to check with Clermont County Shelter and Heartland Christian Church food pantry for National Night Out donations
key_amounts: $41,761 - employee retention grant awarded to fire department
$47,239.85 - transfer from Levy Fund 2193 to Firehouse Construction Fund 4901 for Station 18 consulting/architectural work
4.4 mil - fire and EMS tax levy to be placed on ballot
$10 per ton decrease in road salt price this year
notable_topics: HR policy revisions for EEO, ADA, and Pregnancy Worker Fairness Act compliance
Station 18 design development completion and upcoming hard bid process
4.4 mil fire and EMS tax levy for ballot
Memorandum of understanding with IAFF Local 3932 regarding paramedic staffing
Donation of surplus firefighter gear rack to Monroe Township Fire Department
OPWC grant pursuit for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving
Road salt purchase through SWAP 4G program with Cargill Salt
CPAAA fundraiser at Sprinkles and National Night Out Stuff the Cruiser event
notes: Meeting was brief, adjourning at 6:18 PM. Invocation referenced the Allen family's loss and national events from the prior week. Station 18 hard bid dates set for July 30 and August 8 with presentation planned for August 13. Transcript had significant portions of garbled/corrupted text likely due to audio transcription issues.
title: BOT 7-16-24
